name: Cetirizine
notes: Second-generation antihistamine; non-sedating for most; drowsiness possible

name: Loratadine
notes: Least sedating OTC antihistamine; 24-hour relief

name: Fexofenadine
intakeConditions: with water; avoid fruit juice which reduces absorption
notes: Non-sedating antihistamine; fruit juice significantly reduces absorption — use water

name: Diphenhydramine
notes: Sedating first-generation antihistamine; not for regular long-term use; anticholinergic effects

name: Chlorpheniramine
notes: Sedating first-gen antihistamine; often combined in cold medicines

name: Levocetirizine
notes: Active enantiomer of cetirizine; slightly more potent; may cause more sedation than loratadine

name: Omeprazole
notes: Proton pump inhibitor; long-term use may deplete B12, magnesium; taper if discontinuing

name: Esomeprazole
notes: PPI; S-enantiomer of omeprazole; similar risks with long-term use

name: Pantoprazole
notes: Prescription PPI; frequently used for erosive esophagitis and GERD

name: Famotidine
notes: H2 blocker; safer long-term than PPIs for most people; good for nighttime reflux

name: Ranitidine
notes: H2 blocker; market recall due to NDMA contamination; largely replaced by famotidine

name: Calcium Carbonate Antacid
intakeConditions: chew thoroughly; do not swallow whole
notes: Fast-acting antacid; also calcium supplement; do not exceed 7.5g daily

name: Simethicone
intakeConditions: after meals and at bedtime as needed
notes: Anti-gas agent; breaks up gas bubbles; safe and not absorbed systemically

name: Bismuth Subsalicylate
notes: Antidiarrheal and antacid; may darken stools and tongue; contains salicylate — avoid if salicylate sensitive

name: Loperamide
notes: Antidiarrheal; slows gut motility; do not use for infectious diarrhea with fever; max 8mg/day OTC

name: Polyethylene Glycol 3350
notes: Osmotic laxative; works by holding water in colon; generally well tolerated; onset 1-3 days

name: Milk of Magnesia
notes: Osmotic laxative and antacid; works in 6-8 hours; magnesium absorbed systemically

name: Docusate Sodium
notes: Stool softener; allows water into stool; gentle for postpartum and surgery recovery

name: Psyllium Husk OTC
intakeConditions: with 8 oz of water — choking hazard without
notes: Bulk-forming laxative; also lowers LDL cholesterol; FDA approved health claim

name: Methylcellulose
notes: Non-fermentable fiber; less bloating than psyllium; FDA approved bulk-forming laxative

name: Acetaminophen
intakeConditions: with or without food; limit alcohol use
notes: Analgesic and antipyretic; max 3-4g/day; liver toxicity risk with alcohol or overdose

name: Ibuprofen
intakeConditions: with food or milk to reduce GI upset
notes: NSAID analgesic/anti-inflammatory; cardiovascular and GI risks with prolonged use; avoid in kidney disease

name: Naproxen
notes: Longer-acting NSAID; 12-hour relief; same GI/cardiovascular cautions as ibuprofen

name: Aspirin
notes: NSAID; antiplatelet at low dose; used in cardiovascular prevention; GI risk; avoid in children (Reye's)

name: Acetaminophen + Caffeine
notes: Combination analgesic for migraines; caffeine enhances pain relief; limit to 2 days/week to prevent rebound headache

name: Pseudoephedrine
intakeConditions: with or without food; take early to avoid insomnia
notes: Oral decongestant; behind pharmacy counter; avoid with hypertension or heart disease

name: Phenylephrine
notes: Oral decongestant; FDA advisory questioned its efficacy; less effective than pseudoephedrine

name: Oxymetazoline Nasal Spray
intakeConditions: do not tilt head back; breathe in gently
notes: Topical nasal decongestant; do not use more than 3 days — risk of rebound congestion (rhinitis medicamentosa)

name: Guaifenesin
notes: Expectorant; thins mucus; increase fluid intake for best effect

name: Doxylamine
notes: Sedating antihistamine used as sleep aid; also used with B6 for pregnancy nausea (Diclegis/Bonjesta)

name: Melatonin OTC
notes: Circadian hormone; start at lowest effective dose (0.5-1mg); not habit-forming

name: Diphenhydramine Sleep Aid
notes: OTC sleep aid; anticholinergic; not for regular long-term use; may cause next-day grogginess

name: Hydrocortisone Cream 1%
intakeConditions: apply to affected area 1-4x daily; do not use on face long-term
notes: Mild topical corticosteroid; itch and inflammation relief; avoid long-term facial use

name: Clotrimazole
notes: Topical antifungal; athlete's foot, ringworm, jock itch; use full 4-week course

name: Miconazole
notes: Vaginal antifungal for yeast infections; 1-7 day regimens available OTC

name: Bacitracin
intakeConditions: apply to clean wound; cover with bandage
notes: Topical antibiotic; first aid for minor cuts; less allergenic than neomycin

name: Neosporin
notes: Triple antibiotic combination; common contact sensitizer (neomycin); bacitracin alone preferred by some

name: Benzoyl Peroxide
intakeConditions: apply to clean dry skin; may bleach fabrics
notes: OTC acne treatment; kills C. acnes bacteria; start with low concentration (2.5%)

name: Salicylic Acid Topical
notes: Beta-hydroxy acid; exfoliates pores; acne and keratosis pilaris treatment

name: Tretinoin
intakeConditions: apply to dry skin 20-30 min after washing; use sunscreen daily
notes: PRESCRIPTION: vitamin A derivative; gold standard anti-aging and acne treatment; initial purging common

name: Azelaic Acid
notes: Rx and OTC formulations; rosacea, hyperpigmentation, acne; gentler than tretinoin; pregnancy category B

name: Artificial Tears
intakeConditions: tilt head back; apply to lower conjunctival sac
notes: OTC lubricating drops for dry eyes; preservative-free single-use recommended for frequent use

name: Ketotifen Eye Drops
intakeConditions: wait 10 min after contact lens insertion
notes: OTC antihistamine eye drops; allergic conjunctivitis relief; safe for daily use

name: Olopatadine Eye Drops
intakeConditions: do not touch eye with dropper
notes: OTC mast cell stabilizer and antihistamine eye drops; once-daily formulation available

name: Ciprofloxacin Ear Drops
intakeConditions: warm drops in hand before use; lie with ear up for 30-60 sec
notes: PRESCRIPTION: antibiotic ear drops for otitis externa (swimmer's ear) and middle ear infection

name: NyQuil
intakeConditions: do not take with other APAP products
notes: Combination cold/flu nighttime medicine; contains acetaminophen, DXM, and doxylamine

name: DayQuil
intakeConditions: do not take with other APAP products
notes: Daytime cold/flu combination; no antihistamine so does not cause drowsiness

name: Zinc Acetate Lozenges
intakeConditions: dissolve slowly in mouth; do not swallow; use every 2-3 hours
notes: Start within 24h of cold onset; do not use nasal spray form (anosmia risk); good evidence for symptom reduction

name: Oscillococcinum
intakeConditions: dissolve under tongue 15 min before or after eating
notes: Homeopathic preparation; clinical evidence limited; widely used in Europe; considered safe

name: Prenatal Vitamin OTC
notes: OTC prenatal; verify includes folate/methylfolate, iron, DHA, iodine; quality varies widely by brand

name: Ginger Capsules
notes: Safe for pregnancy nausea; take 4x daily at 250mg; comparable to vitamin B6 for nausea

name: Unisom + B6 Protocol
intakeConditions: take at bedtime; may add additional daytime doses if needed
notes: First-line recommendation for pregnancy nausea; can add half-tablet in AM and PM if needed

name: Colace Prenatal
notes: Safe stool softener in pregnancy and postpartum; helps prevent straining

name: Metformin
intakeConditions: with food to minimize GI side effects
notes: PRESCRIPTION: type 2 diabetes and PCOS; off-label longevity use; may deplete B12; AMPK activator

name: Levothyroxine
intakeConditions: on empty stomach 30-60 min before breakfast; separate from calcium, iron, coffee
notes: PRESCRIPTION: synthetic T4; most prescribed thyroid medication; requires T4 to T3 conversion

name: Liothyronine
notes: PRESCRIPTION: synthetic T3; active thyroid hormone; used when T4 conversion is impaired

name: Low Dose Naltrexone
intakeConditions: at bedtime (10pm-2am for best endorphin effect)
notes: PRESCRIPTION: off-label immune modulator; used for autoimmune conditions, pain, and cancer adjunct

name: Progesterone Oral
intakeConditions: take orally at bedtime; food increases absorption
notes: PRESCRIPTION: bioidentical micronized progesterone; sleep and calming effects; luteal phase and HRT use

name: Progesterone Vaginal
notes: PRESCRIPTION: vaginal route avoids first-pass metabolism; ART protocols and luteal support

name: Estradiol Patch
intakeConditions: apply to clean dry skin on lower abdomen or buttocks; rotate sites
notes: PRESCRIPTION: transdermal delivery avoids liver first-pass; lower clot risk than oral estrogen

name: Estradiol Cream
intakeConditions: apply as directed to vaginal area or skin
notes: PRESCRIPTION: vaginal application for atrophy; topical for systemic HRT

name: Testosterone Cypionate
notes: PRESCRIPTION: TRT for men and women (lower dose); monitored with serum testosterone levels

name: Testosterone Cream
intakeConditions: apply to inner wrists or upper arm; wash hands
notes: PRESCRIPTION: compounded testosterone; used in TRT and female hypoactive sexual desire

name: DHEA Rx
notes: PRESCRIPTION: vaginal DHEA for atrophy; also used as precursor in hormone cascade

name: Hydrocortisone Low Dose Oral
intakeConditions: mimic diurnal cortisol: larger dose AM, smaller dose noon
notes: PRESCRIPTION: physiological dose cortisol; used in adrenal insufficiency and functional medicine protocols

name: Desiccated Thyroid
intakeConditions: on empty stomach 30-60 min before breakfast
notes: PRESCRIPTION: porcine thyroid extract containing T4 and T3; preferred by some functional medicine MDs

name: Ivermectin
notes: PRESCRIPTION: antiparasitic; used off-label in some functional protocols; weight-based dosing

name: Hydroxychloroquine
notes: PRESCRIPTION: autoimmune (lupus, RA) and antimalarial; monitor ophthalmology annually

name: Spironolactone
notes: PRESCRIPTION: aldosterone antagonist; used for acne, hormonal hair loss, PCOS, and blood pressure

name: Finasteride
notes: PRESCRIPTION: 5-AR inhibitor; reduces DHT; hair loss and BPH; side effects include sexual dysfunction

name: Minoxidil
intakeConditions: apply to dry scalp (topical); take with or without food (oral)
notes: OTC topical; Rx oral (low dose off-label); vasodilator; stimulates hair follicles; apply consistently

name: Modafinil
intakeConditions: in morning or early afternoon; avoid after noon
notes: PRESCRIPTION: eugeroic; used for narcolepsy, shift work sleep disorder, and off-label cognitive enhancement

name: Armodafinil
notes: PRESCRIPTION: R-enantiomer of modafinil; longer-acting; used for wakefulness

name: Amphetamine Salts
intakeConditions: with or without food; take early to avoid insomnia
notes: PRESCRIPTION Schedule II: ADHD treatment; stimulant; Schedule II controlled substance

name: Methylphenidate
notes: PRESCRIPTION Schedule II: ADHD stimulant; shorter acting than amphetamines; multiple daily doses often needed

name: Lisdexamfetamine
intakeConditions: with or without food in morning
notes: PRESCRIPTION Schedule II: prodrug; smooth extended release; ADHD and BED treatment

name: Naltrexone Full Dose
notes: PRESCRIPTION: opioid and alcohol use disorder treatment; different mechanism from LDN at 1/10 the dose

name: Clomiphene
intakeConditions: take days 3-7 or 5-9 of cycle as directed
notes: PRESCRIPTION: selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM); ovulation induction; also used in male hypogonadism

name: Letrozole
notes: PRESCRIPTION: aromatase inhibitor; preferred over Clomid for ovulation induction in PCOS; also TRT adjunct

name: Progesterone Compounded
notes: PRESCRIPTION: compounded bioidentical progesterone; customized dosing from compounding pharmacy

name: Biest Cream
intakeConditions: apply to thin-skinned areas; rotate sites
notes: PRESCRIPTION: compounded combination of estradiol (E2) and estriol (E3); 80/20 or 50/50 ratio common

name: Triest Cream
notes: PRESCRIPTION: compounded combination of E1 (estrone), E2 (estradiol), and E3 (estriol); less common than biest

name: Prednisone
intakeConditions: with food to reduce GI upset
notes: PRESCRIPTION: corticosteroid; taper when discontinuing; short-term for inflammation; long-term has significant risks

name: Methylprednisolone
notes: PRESCRIPTION: more potent corticosteroid; dose packs provide tapering schedule

name: Methotrexate
intakeConditions: take with folate supplement; avoid alcohol
notes: PRESCRIPTION: DMARDs for autoimmune conditions; must supplement with folate; liver monitoring required

name: Hydroxyprogesterone Caproate
intakeConditions: intramuscular injection in upper outer thigh
notes: PRESCRIPTION: prevents preterm birth in high-risk pregnancies; weekly injections from weeks 16-36

name: Drospirenone + Ethinyl Estradiol
notes: PRESCRIPTION: combined oral contraceptive; antimineralocorticoid; used for acne and PMDD

name: Norethindrone
intakeConditions: same time each day within 3-hour window (progestin-only)
notes: PRESCRIPTION: progestin-only contraceptive or hormone therapy; safe for breastfeeding

name: Valacyclovir
notes: PRESCRIPTION: antiviral for herpes simplex, herpes zoster; suppressive therapy for recurrent outbreaks

name: Doxycycline
intakeConditions: with full glass of water; with food if GI upset; avoid lying down after
notes: PRESCRIPTION: tetracycline antibiotic; acne, Lyme disease, tick-borne illness, malaria prophylaxis

name: Azithromycin
notes: PRESCRIPTION: macrolide antibiotic; respiratory, STI, and atypical infections; Z-pack = 5-day course

name: Amoxicillin
notes: PRESCRIPTION: penicillin-class antibiotic; common for dental, strep, ear infections

name: Nystatin
intakeConditions: swish and swallow (oral) or apply topically
notes: PRESCRIPTION: antifungal for candida; not absorbed orally so safe; topical and oral suspension forms

name: Fluconazole
notes: PRESCRIPTION: oral antifungal; single dose for yeast infection; longer course for systemic candida

name: Itraconazole
intakeConditions: with full meal; acidic environment improves absorption
notes: PRESCRIPTION: broad-spectrum antifungal; used for systemic fungal and mold exposure

name: Amphotericin B Nasal Spray
notes: PRESCRIPTION: compounded antifungal nasal spray; used in mold illness protocols

name: Cholestyramine
intakeConditions: mixed in water or juice; separate from medications by 4-6 hours
notes: PRESCRIPTION: bile acid sequestrant; used in mold/biotoxin illness protocols (Shoemaker) and for cholesterol

name: Rifaximin
notes: PRESCRIPTION: non-absorbed antibiotic; used for SIBO (hydrogen), IBS-D, and traveler's diarrhea

name: Neomycin
notes: PRESCRIPTION: non-absorbed antibiotic for methane-dominant SIBO; often combined with rifaximin

name: Budesonide
intakeConditions: swallow whole; do not crush or chew
notes: PRESCRIPTION: topically-acting corticosteroid; less systemic absorption; IBD (Crohn's, microscopic colitis)

name: Mesalamine
notes: PRESCRIPTION: aminosalicylate for IBD; local anti-inflammatory; UC and Crohn's maintenance

name: Sertraline
intakeConditions: with or without food; take same time daily
notes: PRESCRIPTION: SSRI antidepressant; first-line for depression, OCD, PTSD, panic, and anxiety

name: Escitalopram
notes: PRESCRIPTION: SSRI; fewer drug interactions than many SSRIs; commonly prescribed for anxiety and depression

name: Bupropion
intakeConditions: with food; avoid if seizure history
notes: PRESCRIPTION: NDRI antidepressant; also for smoking cessation; less sexual dysfunction than SSRIs

name: Buspirone
intakeConditions: with food consistently — grapefruit increases levels
notes: PRESCRIPTION: non-benzodiazepine anxiolytic; takes 2-4 weeks; no dependence or withdrawal

name: Clonazepam
notes: PRESCRIPTION Schedule IV: long-acting benzodiazepine; anxiety and seizure; risk of dependence

name: Trazodone
intakeConditions: with small snack to reduce dizziness
notes: PRESCRIPTION: antidepressant used off-label for sleep; sedating at low doses; no significant dependence

name: Mirtazapine
notes: PRESCRIPTION: antidepressant with strong sedating and appetite-stimulating effects; useful for insomnia/poor appetite

name: Naltrexone + Bupropion
intakeConditions: do not crush; swallow whole; avoid with high fat meal
notes: PRESCRIPTION: combination for chronic weight management; titrate over 4 weeks to target dose

name: Semaglutide
intakeConditions: subcutaneous injection; any time of day; rotate injection sites
notes: PRESCRIPTION: GLP-1 agonist; diabetes and weight management; nausea common; titrate slowly

name: Tirzepatide
intakeConditions: subcutaneous injection any time of day
notes: PRESCRIPTION: dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist; significant weight loss; greater efficacy than semaglutide in trials

name: Liraglutide
notes: PRESCRIPTION: GLP-1 agonist; daily injection; predecessor to semaglutide

name: Testosterone Gel
intakeConditions: apply to upper arm/shoulder; wash hands; cover to prevent transfer
notes: PRESCRIPTION: TRT for men; transdermal delivery; transfer risk to partners and children

name: HCG
notes: PRESCRIPTION: LH mimic; used to maintain fertility and testicular function during TRT

name: Anastrozole
notes: PRESCRIPTION: aromatase inhibitor; used to manage high estrogen during TRT; breast cancer treatment

name: Tamoxifen
notes: PRESCRIPTION: SERM; breast cancer prevention and treatment; used off-label for male gynecomastia during TRT

name: Pioglitazone
notes: PRESCRIPTION: thiazolidinedione; insulin sensitizer; NASH and PCOS interest

name: Acarbose
intakeConditions: with first bite of each meal
notes: PRESCRIPTION: alpha-glucosidase inhibitor; blunts postprandial glucose spikes; GI side effects common

name: Naltrexone Implant
notes: PRESCRIPTION: subcutaneous implant releasing naltrexone over 2-3 months; opioid and alcohol use disorder

name: Desmopressin
intakeConditions: limit fluid intake from 1 hour before to 8 hours after
notes: PRESCRIPTION: vasopressin analogue; nocturnal enuresis, diabetes insipidus; hyponatremia risk if overhydration

name: Gabapentin
notes: PRESCRIPTION: off-label for neuropathic pain, sleep, anxiety, and restless legs; dependence possible

name: Pregabalin
notes: PRESCRIPTION Schedule V: fibromyalgia, neuropathy, generalized anxiety; sedating; withdrawal risk

name: Colchicine
notes: PRESCRIPTION: anti-gout and anti-inflammatory; also pericarditis; narrow therapeutic window

name: Allopurinol
intakeConditions: with food to reduce GI effects; increase water intake
notes: PRESCRIPTION: lowers uric acid; gout prevention; start low to avoid flares

name: Amlodipine
notes: PRESCRIPTION: dihydropyridine CCB; hypertension and angina; peripheral edema common side effect

name: Lisinopril
notes: PRESCRIPTION: ACE inhibitor; hypertension, heart failure, diabetic nephropathy; dry cough side effect

name: Losartan
notes: PRESCRIPTION: ARB; alternative to ACE inhibitors without cough; hypertension and kidney protection

name: Atorvastatin
intakeConditions: with or without food; take in evening
notes: PRESCRIPTION: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor; LDL lowering; CoQ10 depletion; muscle pain side effect

name: Rosuvastatin
intakeConditions: with or without food; any time of day
notes: PRESCRIPTION: potent statin; lower myopathy risk than other statins; also raises HDL

name: Ezetimibe
notes: PRESCRIPTION: cholesterol absorption inhibitor; often combined with statins; Vytorin = ezetimibe + simvastatin

name: Berberine Rx Strength
notes: PRESCRIPTION strength dosing; AMPK activator; blood sugar and lipid effects; drug interactions possible

name: Montelukast
notes: PRESCRIPTION: leukotriene antagonist; asthma and allergic rhinitis; neuropsychiatric side effects possible

name: Fluticasone Nasal Spray
intakeConditions: direct spray away from nasal septum
notes: OTC and Rx: intranasal corticosteroid; preferred first-line for allergic rhinitis

name: Mometasone Nasal Spray
intakeConditions: spray into nostril; tilt head slightly forward
notes: PRESCRIPTION: potent intranasal corticosteroid; allergic rhinitis and nasal polyps

name: Budesonide Nasal Spray
notes: OTC and Rx: intranasal corticosteroid; safe in pregnancy; good for allergic rhinitis

name: Ipratropium Nasal Spray
notes: PRESCRIPTION: anticholinergic nasal spray; non-allergic rhinitis and runny nose (vasomotor)

name: Azelastine Nasal Spray
notes: OTC and Rx: antihistamine nasal spray; fast-acting allergy relief; may cause drowsiness; bitter taste

name: Tiotropium
notes: PRESCRIPTION: COPD maintenance inhaler; once-daily long-acting bronchodilator

name: Albuterol
intakeConditions: shake before use; rinse mouth after
notes: PRESCRIPTION: short-acting beta2-agonist; bronchodilator for asthma and exercise-induced bronchospasm

name: Fluticasone Inhaler
intakeConditions: rinse mouth after use to prevent thrush
notes: PRESCRIPTION: inhaled corticosteroid; asthma maintenance; use spacer for better delivery

name: Vitamin D3 Prescription
notes: PRESCRIPTION dose: used to correct severe vitamin D deficiency; D2 form; monitor 25-OH-D levels
